items.final_wording: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

items.rationale: Supports a structured clinician-led medication review without pregnancy-specific safety conclusions.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.5 — involvement, values, preferences, benefits, and harms

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.

items.rationale: Supports documented preferences, benefits and harms discussion.

items.final_wording: Defines reconciliation as identifying an accurate current medicine list, comparing records, recognising discrepancies, documenting changes, and including over-the-counter or complementary medicines.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines reconciliation as identifying an accurate current medicine list, comparing records, recognising discrepancies, documenting changes, and including over-the-counter or complementary medicines.

items.rationale: Supports accurate reconciliation of entered medicines.

limitations: Full source text was retrieved and extracted, but section-level clinical synthesis remains conservative; no dose, threshold, timing, or population claim is inferred beyond the inspected source record.
Workflows remain documented-limitation outputs until every applicable section is fully reconstructed.
